domain: gecko setae adhesion (hierarchical lamellae→setae→spatulae × ~1M setae × ~100 spatulae; van der Waals force activated only under perpendicular preload + tangential drag at correct angle; hyperextension reverses angle for clean directional release)
specific_mechanism: vdW force is omnipresent but adhesion manifests only when (1) perpendicular preload engages spatulae against surface and (2) tangential drag aligns spatulae for maximum contact area. Detachment is achieved by reversing the angle (hyperextension), not by overcoming force. Massive total adhesion arises from million-fold spatula multiplication, not chemistry.
llm_application: Preload-and-drag gated adapter coupling (PDGAC): hierarchical nano-adapter array (1024 per layer, rank=2 each) gated by a two-step contact protocol. (1) PRELOAD: a perpendicular selector activates only head-subspaces aligned with input low-frequency content. (2) DRAG: a tangential adapter pass injects update only for activated nano-adapters at the preload-determined angle. Reverse-angle inputs (cosine sign flip) trigger release. Two-step asymmetric engagement yields high coupling per parameter with clean directional release.
novelty_claim: HILO (2502.03884) hierarchical adapter rank does not gate on two-step preload+drag. Generative Adapter (2411.05877) is single-pass. Multi-Grained Patch Training (2501.15087) is granularity-only. MoE routing is single-step. The asymmetric two-axis preload+drag gating with reverse-angle release is not retrieved.
